twitter_thread: Passive Income matters more than people think. Here's why → 🧵
1/ The default approach to Passive Income wastes time and money.
2/ The cause: most resources oversimplify or assume too much.
3/ The fix is three moves: survey, default, iterate.
4/ Survey: find the 3 leading options, ignore long-tail.
5/ Default: pick one that covers 80% of cases.
6/ Iterate: time-box a trial, measure honestly.
7/ That's it. Save this thread for later.
pack.linkedin:
Passive Income is one of those topics where most takes are either too generic or too in-the-weeds.

Here is a framework that has worked in practice: start with a survey, pick a default, iterate on a trial window. The point is to make the first decision quickly, then learn from doing.

If you are evaluating Passive Income right now and feeling stuck, the cheapest mistake is overthinking. Pick the option that covers the most cases, set a date when you will review it, and start.

What is your current default?
tiktok_hooks: Stop overthinking Passive Income — here's the 3-move framework

pack.reddit_headline: I spent 6 months testing Passive Income so you don't have to. Here's what actually worked.
